theme: balance a chemist's scale with signed reference weights
title: Chemist's Scale: Signed Weighing Design
strategies: sliding-window baseline: session i loads references i..i+k-1 all on the left pan (always feasible, badly correlated)

row-by-row greedy: build each session by loading the k least-used references so far, sign chosen to cancel the running column sum, plus a small bounded local-swap repair -- never reconsiders earlier rows

circulant + quadratic-residue construction: pick a prime p=k with p = 3 (mod 4), border a p x p Jacobsthal circulant (first row = Legendre symbol of the shift) with an all-ones row/column to get a (p+1) x (p+1) block with EXACT zero defect; tile n // (p+1) disjoint copies of this block down the diagonal

two-block patching: when n is not an exact multiple of p+1, tile as many perfect blocks as fit and cover the leftover rows with a sliding-window fallback so the design stays feasible even where no perfect weighing matrix embeds
